01_initial_request.input_data: Tandem mass spectrometry data (MS1 and MS2 spectra) from Data-Dependent Acquisition (DDA) and Wide-Window Acquisition (WWA) proteomics experiments
01_initial_request.user_intent: To develop a database search algorithm that enhances peptide identification sensitivity by detecting co-fragmented peptides in chimeric spectra neglected by traditional tools and to support efficient analysis of Wide-Window Acquisition data
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Significantly increase the number of identified peptide sequences and proteins while maintaining a 1% False Discovery Rate (FDR) and reducing computational runtime compared to existing tools

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Traditional DDA tools typically identify a single peptide per spectrum, neglecting co-fragmented peptides in chimeric spectra. [Gap] There is a need for an algorithm that can detect co-fragmented peptides with high sensitivity without relying on high-quality MS1 precursor peaks prior to search. [Decision] To achieve enhanced peptide identification, chose to use MSFragger-DDA+ to perform a full isolation window search followed by targeted XIC detection and rescoring on tandem mass spectra, expecting to obtain a comprehensive list of peptide-spectrum matches (PSMs) including co-fragmented peptides.

parameters.rescoring_algorithm: Greedy algorithm for shared fragment removal

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Algorithm successfully detects co-fragmented peptides within the full isolation window and refines PSM scores using targeted precursor signals.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The new algorithm needs validation on standard DDA data to compare sensitivity against existing tools. [Gap] Performance metrics such as peptide identification count and runtime relative to MaxQuant and MetaMorpheus are unknown. [Decision] To evaluate sensitivity, chose to use MSFragger-DDA+ (via FragPipe) to process Searle et al. and Richards et al. DDA datasets, expecting to obtain peptide identification counts at 1% FDR for comparison.

parameters.datasets: Searle et al. (5 HeLa samples, NCE 22-42), Richards et al. (6 HEK 293T samples, Trypsin/AspN/GluC, CID/HCD)
parameters.variable_modifications: Acetylation of protein N-terminus, Oxidation of methionine

02_agent_trajectory.observation: MSFragger-DDA+ identified 57% more peptide sequences than MSFragger DDA mode on average (Searle), and 15-45% more depending on enzyme/fragmentation (Richards). Runtime was four times faster than MaxQuant and MetaMorpheus.

references.relevance: Source of DDA dataset with varying NCE
Source of DDA dataset with varying enzymes and fragmentation

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Increased identification sensitivity must not compromise false discovery rate control. [Gap] The actual FDR of the new method needs empirical verification beyond target-decoy approaches. [Decision] To validate FDR control, chose to use MSFragger-DDA+ to search an entrapment database containing shuffled sequences, expecting to obtain False Discovery Proportion (FDP) estimates.

parameters.database: Entrapment database (target proteins + shuffled entrapment sequences)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: MSFragger-DDA+ maintained a similarly low FDP as MSFragger DDA mode and lower FDP than MaxQuant and MetaMorpheus, confirming stringent FDR control.

references.relevance: Method for calculating False Discovery Proportion (FDP) using entrapment

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The algorithm should perform well on DDA-PASEF data from timsTOF instruments. [Gap] Sensitivity and quantification quality on DDA-PASEF data are unverified. [Decision] To assess performance on timsTOF data, chose to use MSFragger-DDA+ to analyze Wang et al. dataset (A549 and K562 cell lines), expecting to obtain quantified protein counts and CVs.

parameters.datasets: Wang et al. (A549, K562, 3 biological replicates, 4 technical replicates)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: DDA+ workflow quantified 16-19% more proteins without MBR and 11-13% more with MBR compared to conventional DDA workflow. CVs were comparable for common proteins.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Wide-Window Acquisition (WWA) data produces highly multiplexed spectra unsuitable for traditional DDA tools. [Gap] Few tools natively support WWA data analysis with high sensitivity and speed. [Decision] To demonstrate WWA support, chose to use MSFragger-DDA+ to analyze Truong et al. and Matzinger et al. WWA datasets, expecting to obtain peptide identification counts across various isolation windows.

parameters.datasets: Truong et al. (29 low-input samples, 1.6-48 Th windows), Matzinger et al. (43 samples, 1-56 Th windows, 250 pg-400 ng)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: MSFragger-DDA+ showed similar sensitivity to CHIMERYS and higher than MetaMorpheus. It was at least 20 times faster than CHIMERYS coupled with Proteome Discoverer (68.8 min vs 25 h).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Phosphoproteome data involves larger search spaces and modification localization challenges. [Gap] It is unclear if the co-fragmentation detection benefits phosphosite identification. [Decision] To evaluate phosphoproteome performance, chose to use MSFragger-DDA+ to analyze Sacco et al. phosphorylation-enriched dataset, expecting to obtain phosphosite and phosphosequence counts.

parameters.dataset: Sacco et al. (6 replicates, phosphorylation-enriched human samples)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: MSFragger-DDA+ identified ~5% more phosphosites and ~14% more phosphosequences than MSFragger DDA mode.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The ultimate goal is to enable more comprehensive analysis of clinical proteomics data. [Gap] The impact on downstream differential expression analysis needs demonstration. [Decision] To validate clinical utility, chose to use FragPipe-Analyst to process MSFragger-DDA+ results from the Glioma dataset, expecting to obtain differentially expressed genes and pathway enrichment.

parameters.dataset: Glioma study (42 samples, IDHmut vs IDHwt)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: DDA+ workflow identified 935 more proteins than MSFragger DDA, leading to detection of more differentially expressed genes and enriched GO categories.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Benchmarking against established tools (MaxQuant, MetaMorpheus, CHIMERYS) across diverse datasets (DDA, DDA-PASEF, WWA, Phospho, Clinical) with entrapment database for FDR validation.

peptide_identification_increase_dda.interpretation: Average increase in peptide sequences identified compared to MSFragger DDA mode on Searle et al. dataset.

protein_identification_increase_pasef.interpretation: Increase in quantified proteins on timsTOF DDA-PASEF data without MBR.

runtime_improvement_dda.interpretation: MSFragger-DDA+ coupled with FragPipe is four times faster than MaxQuant and MetaMorpheus.

runtime_improvement_wwa.interpretation: MSFragger-DDA+ was at least 20 times faster than CHIMERYS coupled with Proteome Discoverer on WWA data.

fdr_control.interpretation: Maintained 1% FDR with low False Discovery Proportion (FDP) confirmed by entrapment database search.

glioma_protein_increase.interpretation: More proteins identified compared to MSFragger DDA mode in glioma dataset.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. MSFragger-DDA+ significantly increased peptide and protein identification sensitivity across DDA, DDA-PASEF, and WWA data types while maintaining 1% FDR and reducing computational runtime compared to existing tools.
